description: Providers tab renders Microsoft card alongside existing Google card.
Microsoft settings status action returns success for authorized internal admin user.
Microsoft settings status action returns masked values only (no raw secret content).
Microsoft settings save action rejects empty client ID.
Microsoft settings save action rejects empty client secret.
Microsoft settings save action defaults tenant ID to `common` when omitted.
Microsoft settings save action persists `microsoft_client_id` in tenant secrets.
Microsoft settings save action persists `microsoft_client_secret` in tenant secrets.
Microsoft settings save action persists `microsoft_tenant_id` in tenant secrets.
Microsoft settings status action exposes derived redirect URI and scope metadata.
Microsoft settings reset action disconnects Microsoft email providers for the tenant.
Microsoft settings reset action disconnects Microsoft calendar providers for the tenant.
Microsoft settings save/reset actions are exported via integrations action index and callable from UI imports.
Non-admin user receives permission error on Microsoft settings save.
Client-portal user context is denied on Microsoft settings status/save/reset actions.
Microsoft readiness helper returns ready only when both `microsoft_client_id` and `microsoft_client_secret` exist.
Google readiness helper returns ready only when both `google_client_id` and `google_client_secret` exist.
CE Microsoft email form no longer blocks save due to manual client credentials fields.
CE Microsoft email form shows Providers CTA when Microsoft readiness is false.
CE Microsoft calendar form shows Providers CTA when Microsoft readiness is false.
CE Microsoft calendar form can save provider metadata without manual OAuth credential entry.
CE Microsoft email provider persistence writes null/derived credential fields rather than requiring form-entered secrets.
Google provider readiness for MSP SSO uses `google_client_id`/`google_client_secret` and does not require Gmail PubSub keys.
CE MSP login renders Google and Microsoft SSO buttons from non-stub implementation.
SSO buttons remain disabled until email input is non-empty.
Microsoft button triggers resolver call before invoking NextAuth signIn.
Google button triggers resolver call before invoking NextAuth signIn.
Resolver failure always shows same generic error message text in MSP login UI.
Client portal login UI remains unchanged and does not render new SSO buttons.
Resolver endpoint accepts valid payload and returns `{ ok: true }` with context cookie when source is resolvable.
Resolver endpoint rejects invalid provider values with generic failure response shape.
Resolver endpoint normalizes email case/whitespace before lookup.
Resolver selects tenant source for Microsoft when user exists and tenant Microsoft secrets are present.
Resolver selects tenant source for Google when user exists and tenant Google secrets are present.
Resolver selects app fallback source when user exists but tenant Microsoft config is missing and app fallback exists.
Resolver selects app fallback source when user exists but tenant Google config is missing and app fallback exists.
Resolver unknown-user path with available app fallback returns same success schema as known-user-missing-provider path.
Resolver unknown-user path with no available fallback returns same generic failure schema as known-user-no-source path.
Resolver context cookie payload excludes raw client IDs and client secrets.
Resolver context cookie includes provider, source, issuedAt/expiresAt, nonce, and signature.
Resolver uses `MICROSOFT_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID` + `MICROSOFT_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET` for Microsoft fallback readiness check.
Resolver uses `GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID` + `GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET` for Google fallback readiness check.
Resolver returns generic failure when tenant source missing and fallback source missing.
Resolver rate limiter blocks repeated abusive attempts and returns generic failure response.
Resolver structured logs include provider and source classification but no raw email, secrets, or explicit existence marker.
CE build registers Google/Microsoft OAuth providers in NextAuth when fallback or tenant-selected source is available.
Auth options are not stuck on stale provider secrets across attempts with different resolver cookies.
NextAuth secret resolver uses tenant source from valid resolver cookie for Microsoft.
NextAuth secret resolver uses tenant source from valid resolver cookie for Google.
Invalid resolver cookie signature is ignored and app fallback is used.
Expired resolver cookie context is ignored and app fallback is used.
Resolver cookie is overwritten on subsequent SSO start attempts (new nonce and expiry).
CE OAuth mapper resolves internal user by normalized email for Microsoft profile and returns expected extended user shape.
CE OAuth mapper resolves internal user by normalized email for Google profile and returns expected extended user shape.
CE OAuth mapper rejects inactive user accounts.
CE OAuth mapper rejects client user_type for MSP SSO flow.
EE build path continues to use enterprise registry profile mapper unchanged.
CE MSP OAuth sign-in succeeds without EE account-link persistence dependencies.
Microsoft OAuth issuer/authorization path uses tenant ID when provided, else defaults to `common`.
Code comments/docs include explicit anti-enumeration guidance in resolver/auth flow modules.
`.env.example` documents CE MSP fallback usage for `GOOGLE_OAUTH_*` and `MICROSOFT_OAUTH_*` keys.
Integration docs describe provider setup order for Microsoft and Google in Providers settings before account connection flows.
DB-backed integration sanity (happy path): resolver selects tenant source when matching internal user row and tenant secret readiness are present.
DB-backed integration sanity (guard path): resolver for unknown email returns generic response without user-existence details.
DB-backed integration sanity (fallback path): resolver selects app source when user row exists but tenant readiness is absent.
End-to-end MSP Microsoft SSO with tenant source succeeds from login form to authenticated redirect.
End-to-end MSP Google SSO with tenant source succeeds from login form to authenticated redirect.
End-to-end MSP Microsoft SSO fallback source succeeds when tenant source is absent but app fallback exists.
End-to-end MSP Google SSO fallback source succeeds when tenant source is absent but app fallback exists.
End-to-end MSP SSO start failure shows same generic UI messaging for unknown user and known-unconfigured tenant.
End-to-end CE credentials login (non-SSO) remains unaffected by resolver cookie behavior.
End-to-end client portal login behavior remains unchanged with no new SSO affordances introduced.
